segments.title: Interplanetary colonization needs logistics hubs
segments.summary: A space-colonization lecture uses maritime logistics hubs, shuttle routes, and repair points to argue against one-stop interplanetary transport schemes.
segments.body: Moon and Martian-orbit staging points, specialized shuttle links, stockpiled supplies, maintenance capacity, and a regular conveyor of ships rather than a single all-purpose craft.

evidence.note: The lecture turns from population scale to age-of-sail port logic, proposing lunar and Martian-orbit staging points instead of a one-stop ship.
The lunar-base section links prior colony-building experience, resource processing, asteroid support, stockpiles, construction equipment, and heavy-lift constraints.
The closing support section compares infrastructure hubs to safer historical voyaging and argues for crew skills, repair points, and a regular ship conveyor with maintenance spares.
